steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage establishes Phoenicians' fame for purple dye, then introduces that their name may have meant “land of purple.” This represents a supporting or reinforcing relationship—the name origin strengthens the claim about purple's importance to their identity.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content: ”In fact” signals reinforcement, where new information supports a previous claim. The name's potential origin strengthens the established claim that the Phoenicians were fundamentally associated with purple dye. The other three choices signal different relationships: “Regardless” signals independence, “Lastly” signals a sequence, and “On the contrary” signals contradiction—none of which fit here. That matches choice A — A is correct.
